headlines.text: Trump lands in Beijing for high-stakes Xi summit as Iran ceasefire hangs by a thread
Iran vows military is at 'highest level of readiness' with 'no room for retreat'
April CPI hits 3.8% — highest since 2023 — as Iran war drives energy costs sky-high
Gunshots erupt inside Philippine Senate as police move to arrest ICC-wanted senator
Pentagon weighing renaming Iran conflict 'Operation Sledgehammer' to reset 60-day war clock
Cannes Film Festival opens with Peter Jackson honorary Palme d'Or, Demi Moore on jury
Nebraska Democratic primary still too close to call with under 300 votes separating candidates
Trump paints National Mall Reflecting Pool 'American Flag Blue' — judge sets May 21 hearing
Amazon launches 'Alexa for Shopping' AI tool and 30-minute-or-less delivery in dozens of cities
Gas prices up 28% year-over-year as Iran war squeezes global oil supply and strains budgets

breaking.text: Gunshots fired inside Philippine Senate as ICC-wanted Senator Dela Rosa faces arrest

stories.hed: Ceasefire on 'life support' as Trump flies to Beijing, Iran says military is ready to fight
stories.dek: Trump called Iran's peace counter-proposal 'totally unacceptable' and boarded Air Force One for China, where he plans a 'long talk' with Xi about a war he insists he doesn't need Beijing's help to end. Iran's military responded by announcing it is at its 'highest level of readiness' — which is a very specific way to say 'we're fine, how are you.'

stories.hed: Gunshots ring out at Philippine Senate as police close in on ICC-wanted senator
stories.dek: Senator Ronald dela Rosa — the architect of Duterte's deadly drug war — barricaded himself inside the Philippine Senate as marines and police moved in to arrest him on ICC crimes against humanity charges. More than a dozen shots were fired, journalists fled, and the building was evacuated, proving that legislative immunity has its limits.

stories.hed: Trump arrives in Beijing to red carpet, military band, and 300 kids waving Chinese flags
stories.dek: China rolled out the full ceremonial treatment for Trump's first Beijing visit in nearly nine years, dispatching Vice President Han Zheng and an honor guard to the tarmac. The agenda includes trade, Taiwan, and whether Xi can help end the Iran war — a war Trump simultaneously says China's help isn't needed for.

stories.hed: Nebraska Democratic primary still too close to call; Schumer warns Trump may 'sell out Taiwan'
stories.dek: With 85% of votes counted, state Sen. John Cavanaugh leads activist Denise Powell by fewer than 300 votes in Nebraska's battleground 2nd District — a race that could help decide House control in November. Meanwhile, Senate Minority Leader Schumer used the Trump-China trip to accuse the president of potentially trading away Taiwan for a headline.

stories.hed: Trump says he 'doesn't think about Americans' financial situation' on Iran — Speaker Johnson scrambles to contextualize
stories.dek: Asked about the economic toll of the Iran war on ordinary Americans, Trump replied that he thinks about only one thing: Iran not having a nuclear weapon. House Speaker Mike Johnson, given a chance to respond, said he was sure the president thinks about Americans' finances — in the tone of a man who is not sure at all.

markets.context: Stocks slid as April PPI surged well above expectations, oil jumped on Iran ceasefire fears, and gold fell as hot inflation priced out any Fed rate cuts this year.

gas.note: Cheaper than CA but pricier than your therapist — and you need both right now.

popCulture.hed: 79th Cannes Film Festival opens with Peter Jackson honorary Palme d'Or and Demi Moore as jury president
popCulture.dek: Peter Jackson received his honorary Palme d'Or from Elijah Wood — a Lord of the Rings reunion nobody knew they needed — while Demi Moore, Jane Fonda, and Heidi Klum lit up a red carpet that Hollywood largely skipped this year. Season 4 of 'The White Lotus' is also filming on location at Cannes itself, which is either very meta or a cry for help.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok trend using the iconic Miranda Priestly line from The Devil Wears Prada sequel to contrast two people, two versions of yourself, or two ways of doing something — one polished, one a disaster. It exploded this week riding the wave of the Devil Wears Prada 2 release and Cannes buzz.
genZ.usage: Trump saying he doesn't think about Americans' financial situation at the gas pump vs. the guy who just paid $6.15 a gallon in California: and Emily... that's all.

tldr: Trump flew to Beijing to charm Xi Jinping into helping end a war he insists he doesn't need China's help to end, while back home inflation hit a three-year high and the average American was paying $4.51 a gallon to fill up their feelings. The Iran ceasefire is technically still alive, in the same way a houseplant you forgot to water for six weeks is technically still alive.
